task: Generate real images for PID control lecture markdown with image placeholders

files_created.description: Block diagram of PID controller showing signal flow: reference r(t) → error e(t) → P/I/D control blocks → sum → plant G(s) → output y(t) with feedback loop
Step response comparison graph showing three configurations: P-only (oscillatory with offset), PI (no offset with overshoot), PID (optimal with minimal overshoot)
Industrial HMI control panel visualization for chemical processing plant showing three PID control loops (temperature, pressure, flow) with real-time status
Python script that generates all three pedagogical diagrams using matplotlib and PIL
Enhanced markdown file with placeholders replaced by actual image references

image_1.content: Diagrama de bloques del sistema de control PID mostrando la señal de referencia r(t), el error e(t), los tres bloques Kp, Ki y Kd en paralelo, la suma de las tres acciones, la planta G(s), la salida y(t) y el lazo de retroalimentación.

image_2.content: Gráfica de respuesta al escalón unitario comparando tres configuraciones: solo P (oscilatorio con offset), PI (sin offset pero con sobrepaso) y PID (respuesta óptima con mínimo sobrepaso). Ejes: tiempo (s) en x, amplitud en y. Incluir la referencia como línea punteada.

image_3.content: Fotografía de un panel de control industrial moderno con pantallas HMI mostrando lazos de control PID en tiempo real, en una planta de procesamiento químico.

implementation_approach.strategy: Python-based image generation using matplotlib and PIL
tools_used: matplotlib.pyplot - for plotting diagrams and graphs
matplotlib.patches - for geometric shapes and annotations
numpy - for numerical calculations and signal generation
PIL (Python Imaging Library) - for image manipulation
techniques_applied: Block diagram construction with custom paths and annotations
Control system response simulation using differential equations
HMI panel visualization with realistic UI elements
High-resolution (300 DPI) output for print-ready quality

execution_log: Step 1: Read source markdown file with image placeholders
Step 2: Analyze placeholder descriptions for image requirements
Step 3: Create Python script for image generation
Step 4: Execute script to generate all three images
Step 5: Create enriched markdown file with proper image references
Step 6: Verify all files created and generate metrics report
notes: All images are pedagogically accurate and designed for engineering undergraduate level
Images are self-contained (not dependent on external libraries or data)
Markdown uses relative paths for maximum portability
High DPI (300) ensures quality when printed or displayed on various media
No external API calls were needed - all content generated locally
Python-only implementation ensures reproducibility without special skills/tools
